name: zod
context: Runtime schema validation and type inference for TypeScript — define once, validate and infer everywhere
info: Zod is the standard runtime validation library for TypeScript. It lets you declare schemas for any data shape — primitives, objects, arrays, unions, discriminated unions, recursive types — and validate unknown input at runtime while inferring static TypeScript types at compile time. Zero dependencies, 2kb gzipped core, works in Node.js and browsers. Agents use Zod constantly: validating API responses, parsing form input, defining tool parameter schemas, enforcing config shapes, and generating TypeScript types from runtime contracts. Zod schemas are immutable and composable — you build complex validators from simple ones using .extend(), .merge(), .pick(), .omit(), .partial(), .transform(), and .pipe(). The ecosystem includes integrations with React Hook Form (via @hookform/resolvers), tRPC, Astro, Remix, Express, and every major TypeScript framework. Zod v4 (current) requires TypeScript >=5.5 with strict mode enabled.

sections.title: Installation and setup
sections.body: Install with `npm install zod`. Requires TypeScript >=5.5 with `strict: true` in tsconfig.json. Import as `import { z } from 'zod'` or `import * as z from 'zod'`. Works in Node.js >=18, Deno, Bun, and modern browsers. No additional configuration needed — Zod is a pure runtime library with zero dependencies.

sections.title: Core API surface
sections.body:
Every Zod schema has these methods:
- `.parse(data)` — validate and return typed data, throws `ZodError` on failure
- `.safeParse(data)` — returns `{ success: true, data }` or `{ success: false, error }` without throwing
- `.parseAsync(data)` / `.safeParseAsync(data)` — async variants for schemas with async refinements or transforms
- `.refine(check, message)` — add custom validation logic
- `.superRefine(check)` — add custom validation with full control over error issues
- `.transform(fn)` — transform validated data into a new shape
- `.default(value)` — provide a default when input is undefined
- `.catch(value)` — provide a fallback when validation fails
- `.optional()` — make the schema accept undefined
- `.nullable()` — make the schema accept null
- `.nullish()` — make the schema accept null or undefined
- `.or(otherSchema)` — create a union
- `.and(otherSchema)` — create an intersection
- `.pipe(otherSchema)` — pipe output into another schema
- `.brand<T>()` — add a nominal type brand
- `.readonly()` — make the inferred type readonly
- `.describe(description)` — add a description for documentation/JSON Schema

sections.title: Ecosystem integrations
sections.body:
Zod integrates with the broader TypeScript ecosystem:
- **tRPC** — uses Zod schemas for input/output validation on API procedures
- **React Hook Form** — `@hookform/resolvers/zod` for form validation
- **Astro** — content collections use Zod schemas for frontmatter validation
- **Remix** — action/loader validation with Zod
- **Express/Fastify** — middleware validation with zod-express-middleware or similar
- **Prisma** — zod-prisma generates Zod schemas from Prisma models
- **OpenAPI** — zod-to-openapi generates OpenAPI specs from Zod schemas
- **JSON Schema** — zod-to-json-schema converts Zod schemas to JSON Schema
- **env validation** — @t3-oss/env-core, znv, zod-env for environment variable validation
